title: Wood Crate K-Cup Holder Drawer
summary: A small two-tier crate with a drilled slide-out tray that holds coffee pods upright — a weekend project sized entirely from scrap plywood.
description:
This is a tiny slatted crate, built the same way a full-size crate is (a 1x2 frame with slats on all four sides), sized specifically to hold a drilled plywood tray of coffee pods that slides in and out like a drawer. The tray isn't a built drawer box — it's a flat panel with holes bored into it at pod-sized spacing, which is what a hole saw is for here, and it simply rests on the crate's internal ledge and pulls straight out.

Size the hole spacing to your actual pods before drilling — pod diameters vary slightly between brands, and a hole cut too tight will crack the plywood around it when you try to seat a pod.

tools.note: Bores the pod holes in the sliding tray — size it to your actual coffee pods before drilling.

materials.name: 1/2" plywood, at least 15" x 14-1/4"
materials.note: Scrap works — buy a new quarter sheet only if you don't have offcuts.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, at least 12-1/2" x 15", two pieces
materials.note: One is the sliding pod tray with holes drilled through it.

cutList.note: Drill pod-sized holes in a grid before installing.

steps.title: Build two 1x2 crate frames
steps.body: Build two rectangular frames from the side, front, and back 1x2 pieces — these form the two tiers of the crate. Glue and nail every joint.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the four legs to one of the frames to raise the crate up, then attach the second frame on top of the legs — you now have a two-tier open crate.

steps.title: Drill the pod tray and add the second crate row
steps.body: Drill a grid of holes sized to your actual coffee pods into one of the 1/4" plywood panels using the hole saw — this becomes the sliding tray. Attach the second row of crate sides above the first if your design uses one, so the tray has a ledge to rest on and slide along.
materials: 1/4" plywood, at least 12-1/2" x 15", two pieces

steps.title: Add the top
steps.body: Attach the 1/2" plywood top to the upper crate frame, then sand every edge — this sits on a counter and gets handled daily.

images.alt: Small wooden crate organizer with a slide-out drilled coffee pod tray
